assertions.reason: `cargo check` succeeded with default features, `cargo check --features blocking` also succeeded, and `cargo test --features blocking --test core_blocking -- --nocapture` passed `blocking_responses_create_matches_async_results_and_metadata` plus `blocking_stream_consumption_matches_async_terminal_output`, showing the blocking facade is opt-in and matches async parsed results, metadata, and stream behavior.

`cargo test --features blocking --test core_blocking -- --nocapture` passed `blocking_responses_create_matches_async_results_and_metadata`, `blocking_stream_consumption_matches_async_terminal_output`, and `blocking_chunked_upload_matches_async_request_sequence`, covering representative create, stream, and chunked upload flows with matching async/blocking request shapes, metadata, and final outputs.

commands.observation: Default-feature cargo check completed successfully and finished the dev profile build for `openai-rust`, establishing the baseline crate surface builds cleanly without enabling `blocking`.
Feature-enabled cargo check completed successfully and finished the dev profile build for `openai-rust`, confirming the crate also compiles when the `blocking` facade is turned on.

`core_blocking` ran 3 tests and all passed: `blocking_responses_create_matches_async_results_and_metadata`, `blocking_stream_consumption_matches_async_terminal_output`, and `blocking_chunked_upload_matches_async_request_sequence`; these outputs directly exercise blocking parity for create, stream, metadata, and upload request sequencing.

summary: All 3 required cargo commands succeeded. VAL-CORE-018 and VAL-CROSS-005 both passed based on successful default/blocking build matrix checks and the 3 passing `core_blocking` parity tests for create, stream, and chunked upload flows.
